challenges.title: Basic Navigation Menu
challenges.description: Create an unordered list (<ul>) of at least three links (<a>) styled as a basic navigation menu. Use a <nav> wrapper and a <style> block that removes list bullets (list-style: none). Assign your code to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a basic navigation menu -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<nav>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <nav> tag'
assert '</nav>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a closing </nav> tag'
assert '<ul>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an unordered list'
assert html.lower().count('<li>') >= 3, 'Should contain at least three list items'
assert html.lower().count('<a ') >= 3, 'Should contain at least three links'
assert 'list-style' in html.lower(), 'Should style list-style to remove bullets'
assert '<style>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <style> block'
hints: Wrap your menu in <nav> for semantic meaning.
Use list-style: none; on the <ul> to remove bullet points.

challenges.title: Horizontal Navigation Menu
challenges.description: Create a horizontal navigation menu using <nav>, <ul>, and <li> with links. Style it with CSS so the items display horizontally (using display: inline, display: inline-block, or display: flex). Add text-decoration and padding styles to the links. Assign your code to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a horizontal navigation menu -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<nav>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <nav> tag'
assert '<ul>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an unordered list'
assert html.lower().count('<li>') >= 3, 'Should contain at least three menu items'
assert html.lower().count('<a ') >= 3, 'Should contain at least three links'
assert '<style>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <style> block'
assert 'list-style' in html.lower(), 'Should remove list bullets'
has_horizontal = 'display: inline' in html.lower() or 'display:inline' in html.lower() or 'display: flex' in html.lower() or 'display:flex' in html.lower()
assert has_horizontal, 'Should use display: inline, inline-block, or flex for horizontal layout'
assert 'text-decoration' in html.lower(), 'Should style text-decoration on links'
assert 'padding' in html.lower(), 'Should add padding for spacing'
hints: Set display: inline or display: inline-block on <li> elements to make them horizontal.
Alternatively, set display: flex on the <ul> for a flexbox-based horizontal layout.
